description: baseline: HEAD af421db + setup, 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh

Cut grounder lookup+alloc overhead: flat-vector MapVariablePosition (single scan, no double lookup), JoinHashTable.clear() instead of realloc in clean_up, reused join key buffer. Behavior-preserving (reference + 62/62 checks). KEEP vs run1 (3.50x, +17.5%) and vs pooled clean baseline (2.71x, +15.2%).

reached_facts+newfacts as reused members (retain buckets across ground()) + hoist join inner-loop head-position lookup. Behavior-preserving (gate passed) but no improvement over run 2 (median 72.4 vs 67.0, DISCARD) and +6MB peak. Once the run-2 wins are in, per-state set/vector allocs and the join hoist are not a dominant cost.

Achievers by-value sink ctor + std::move at join/product sites (kill double vector<int> alloc) + is_cheapest_path lazy_emplace with in-place cost mutation (single hash instead of find+erase+insert). Behavior-preserving (62/62 checks, gate passed). Contemporaneous A/B vs freshly-measured run-2 HEAD: candidate 63.162 vs run-2 63.227 -> 0.1%, confidence 0.02x = NO real gain. The apparent ~6% vs stored run-2 best (67.0) was stored-baseline drift, not a real win. Lesson: grounder micro-allocation cuts don't help past run-2 (glibc tcache); compare candidates against a FRESH contemporaneous HEAD measurement, never stale stored samples.
